perturbation.original_text: Following the completion of such maintenance term, Bankers Bank may, at its option, renew maintenance for subsequent periods of one (1) year each, subject to adjustments proposed by Netzee not to exceed 5% at least sixty (60) days in advance of the applicable renewal date.
perturbation.changed_text: Following the completion of such maintenance term, Netzee may, at its sole discretion, terminate or renew maintenance for subsequent periods of one (1) year each, subject to adjustments proposed by Netzee, which may exceed 5% at any time.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants Bankers Bank the *option* to renew maintenance. The changed text shifts the renewal decision to Netzee's *sole discretion*. This directly contradicts the original clause by removing the bank's right to choose renewal and potentially changing the pricing by exceeding 5% at anytime, creating ambiguity and uncertainty in the enforcement of renewal terms. This undermines the bank's control over the maintenance agreement's continuation and cost.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Following the completion of such maintenance term, Netzee may, at its sole discretion, terminate or renew maintenance for subsequent periods of one (1) year each, subject to adjustments proposed by Netzee, which may exceed 5% at any time. Maintenance provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement shall include (1) standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, and for such purpose Netzee will use commercially reasonable efforts to make and include (without limitation) as such modifications changes and upgrades necessary to comply with generally applicable industry and regulatory requirements of which it is notified (2) 5x12 call-in support between hours of 6:30 a.m. and 6:30 p.m. Eastern Time Monday through Friday, (3) in the event of material discrepancies between the Services as provided and the specifications for such Services (which shall be furnished in reasonably acceptable form), reasonable effort (in relation to importance, impact, and scheduled development plans) to correct those discrepancies, and (4) availability of basic Services with at least 99% uptime between 4:00 a.m Monday and 7:00 p.m. Friday and between 8:00 a.m. Saturday and 2:00 p.m. Saturday Eastern Time, exclusive of outages caused by off-peak prescheduled maintenance or causes beyond Netzee's reasonable control (including third-party equipment or communications).
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants Netzee the sole discretion to renew or terminate maintenance, directly conflicting with the original text that gave Bankers Bank the option to renew. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. The change removes the bank's right to choose renewal and potentially alters pricing by exceeding 5% at any time. This creates a strong in-text contradiction by altering a fundamental right related to renewal options and pricing adjustments, creating uncertainty and potential disputes in enforcement of the renewal terms.

perturbation.original_text: Maintenance provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement shall include (1) standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, and for such purpose Netzee will use commercially reasonable efforts to make and include (without limitation) as such modifications changes and upgrades necessary to comply with generally applicable industry and regulatory requirements of which it is notified (2) 5x12 call-in support between hours of 6:30 a.m. and 6:30 p.m. Eastern Time Monday through Friday, (3) in the event of material discrepancies between the Services as provided and the specifications for such Services (which shall be furnished in reasonably acceptable form), reasonable effort (in relation to importance, impact, and scheduled development plans) to correct those discrepancies, and (4) availability of basic Services with at least 99% uptime between 4:00 a.m Monday and 7:00 p.m. Friday and between 8:00 a.m. Saturday and 2:00 p.m. Saturday Eastern Time, exclusive of outages caused by off-peak prescheduled maintenance or causes beyond Netzee's reasonable control (including third-party equipment or communications).
perturbation.changed_text: Maintenance provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement may include, at Netzee’s discretion, (1) standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to some of its customers, (2) call-in support during unspecified hours and days, (3) effort, if any, to correct discrepancies, and (4) unspecified uptime. Netzee is not responsible for outages.
perturbation.explanation: This modification changes 'shall include' to 'may include, at Netzee's discretion,' fundamentally altering the obligatory nature of maintenance provisions. The initial agreement clearly defines the maintenance services Netzee *must* provide. The revised wording grants Netzee complete discretion over the type and level of maintenance, contradicting the original specifications. It creates uncertainty about what maintenance services will be provided, undermining the purpose of this section and contradicting the original intention of a mandatory standard of service. Furthermore, replacing specific service hours and response commitments with vague terms like 'unspecified hours and days' and 'effort, if any,' removes the enforceability of the original stipulations. Finally, absolving Netzee of any responsibility for outages directly contradicts the earlier commitment to 99% uptime, fostering legal uncertainty about service levels.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Maintenance provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement may include, at Netzee’s discretion, (1) standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to some of its customers, (2) call-in support during unspecified hours and days, (3) effort, if any, to correct discrepancies, and (4) unspecified uptime. Netzee is not responsible for outages. THIS MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT IS GOVERNED BY, AND SHALL BE SUBJECT TO, THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE MASTER AGREEMENT BETWEEN NETZEE AND BANKERS BANK, INCLUDING THE LIMITED WARRANTY AND DISCLAIMER AND LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY PROVIDED THEREIN. SUCH TERMS AND CONDITIONS ARE HEREBY AFFIRMED BY BOTH PARTIES AND INCORPORATED BY REFERENCE IN THIS MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, ALL LICENSES GRANTED OR EXERCISABLE THEREUNDER. COMMENCEMENT DATE FOR MAINTENANCE UNDER THIS ADDENDUM: MARCH 1, 2001 1 IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned duly authorized representatives of the parties hereto have made and entered in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the mandatory nature of maintenance to discretionary, directly contradicting the initial agreement's intent to define required maintenance services. The original text implies an obligation ('shall include'), whereas the modified text gives Netzee complete discretion ('may include, at Netzee's discretion'). This undermines the purpose of the initial section and causes a direct conflict with other parts of the document where the original mandatory standard of service is referenced.
